00_overview.summary: India-Pakistan relations are marked by historical animosity stemming from Partition (1947), territorial disputes (Kashmir), and multiple wars (1947, 1965, 1971, 1999). Diplomatic ties remain minimal with frequent border tensions.
key_facts: Nuclear-armed neighbors with 3,323 km shared border
Kashmir dispute remains core issue since 1947
No official dialogue mechanism active as of 2025

timeline.event: Partition of British India creates India and Pakistan; First Kashmir War begins
timeline.source: Ministry of External Affairs India, Government of Pakistan Archives

timeline.event: Bangladesh Liberation War; Pakistan loses East Pakistan
timeline.source: Hamoodur Rahman Commission Report, MEA India
Kargil Review Committee Report, Pakistan Foreign Office

timeline.event: Indian Parliament attack; military standoff (Operation Parakram)
Mumbai terror attacks (26/11); composite dialogue suspended
timeline.source: National Investigation Agency India, UN Security Council

timeline.event: Pulwama attack; Balakot airstrikes; Article 370 revocation; trade suspended
timeline.source: MEA India, Pakistan Foreign Office, UN reports

diplomatic_missions.india_in_pakistan: High Commission in Islamabad (minimal staff since 2019)
diplomatic_missions.pakistan_in_india: High Commission in New Delhi (minimal staff since 2019)

state_visits.description: PM Narendra Modi surprise visit to Lahore
Pathankot airbase terror attack; dialogue suspended

02_bilateral_ties.current_status: Relations at historic low; no formal dialogue since 2019

treaties.name: Indus Waters Treaty
treaties.significance: Water-sharing agreement; mediated by World Bank; survived all wars
treaties.source: World Bank, MEA India, Pakistan Water & Power Development Authority

treaties.name: Simla Agreement
treaties.significance: Post-1971 war settlement; LoC established in Kashmir

treaties.name: Lahore Declaration
treaties.significance: Peace commitment; violated same year by Kargil War

convergence: Both members of SAARC (South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation)
Both members of Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO)

trade_volume.official_status: All bilateral trade suspended since August 2019
trade_volume.source: UN Comtrade, Reserve Bank of India, State Bank of Pakistan

top_exports: India to Pakistan (pre-2019): Organic chemicals, cotton, petroleum products
top_imports: Pakistan to India (pre-2019): Cement, fresh fruits, leather

trade_potential.source: World Bank South Asia Economic Focus 2024

06_defense_security.border_status: 3,323 km Line of Control (LoC) in Kashmir; frequent ceasefire violations

07_people_to_people.visa_regime: Highly restrictive; only city-specific visas for religious/medical purposes
07_people_to_people.diaspora: Approx 1.5 million Indian diaspora in Pakistan (1947 Partition-era); 1.2 million Pakistani diaspora in India
07_people_to_people.students: No student exchange programs; educational visas rarely granted
07_people_to_people.tourism: Tourism visas virtually non-existent; only religious pilgrims (Kartarpur Corridor for Sikhs)

08_connectivity.air: No direct flights since 2019; all airspace closed to each other's airlines
08_connectivity.rail: Samjhauta Express and Thar Express suspended since 2019
08_connectivity.road: Wagah-Attari border crossing open for trade (suspended 2019); only pedestrian for Kartarpur
08_connectivity.digital: Internet services blocked near border areas during tensions

common_organizations: South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC)
09_multilateral_forums.voting_alignment: Low; often vote oppositely on Kashmir-related UN resolutions
09_multilateral_forums.saarc_status: Stalled since 2016; India boycotted 2016 summit in Islamabad

10_sectoral_cooperation.energy: No cooperation; India blocked Pakistan's participation in TAPI gas pipeline
10_sectoral_cooperation.climate: No joint initiatives despite shared climate vulnerabilities (Himalayan glaciers, Indus basin)
10_sectoral_cooperation.health: Emergency medical visas occasionally granted; no institutional cooperation
10_sectoral_cooperation.agriculture: No cooperation despite shared agricultural challenges (water, pests)

influencers: United States (major arms supplier to Pakistan; strategic partner to India)
China (all-weather ally of Pakistan; border tensions with India)
Saudi Arabia (close to Pakistan; growing ties with India)
proxies: Afghanistan (Pakistan-backed Taliban vs India-backed previous govt)

disputes.title: Kashmir Territorial Dispute
disputes.description: Core issue since 1947; India controls Jammu & Kashmir (revoked autonomy 2019), Pakistan controls Azad Kashmir & Gilgit-Baltistan, China controls Aksai Chin
disputes.source: UN Security Council Resolutions 47 (1948), MEA India, Pakistan Foreign Office

disputes.title: Cross-Border Terrorism
disputes.description: India accuses Pakistan of sponsoring terror groups (Lashkar-e-Taiba, Jaish-e-Mohammed); Pakistan denies allegations
disputes.source: Financial Action Task Force (FATF) reports, US State Dept terrorism reports

disputes.title: Water Disputes
disputes.description: India building dams on western rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab); Pakistan raises objections despite Indus Treaty
disputes.source: World Bank Indus Waters Treaty monitoring

short_term: Status quo likely to continue; no dialogue expected in 2026-27
Border tensions may escalate during election periods
Backchannel diplomacy possible via UAE/Saudi mediation
medium_term: Potential dialogue if leadership changes in either country (2028-30)
Economic pressures may push limited trade resumption
Climate crisis (water scarcity) could force cooperation
long_term: Kashmir issue unlikely to resolve without major geopolitical shift
Generational change may reduce Partition-era animosity (post-2035)
China-US dynamics could realign India-Pakistan calculations
wildcards: Another major terror attack triggering military conflict
Nuclear flashpoint scenario (low probability, catastrophic impact)
Internal instability in Pakistan forcing policy shifts

14_imaginary_peace_scenario.vision: A peaceful, cooperative India-Pakistan relationship would create the world's largest integrated economy (1.6 billion people), unlock $40B annual trade, save $23B/year in defense spending, and transform South Asia into a global growth engine.

steps: Restore diplomatic staff to full strength
Resume Samjhauta Express and bus services
Open 5 additional border crossings for trade
Joint water management commission under Indus Treaty
Grant Most Favored Nation (MFN) status mutually
Open skies agreement for direct flights
LoC declared permanent soft border (open for trade/travel)
Joint administration of disputed territories (like EU Schengen)
Shared sovereignty model inspired by Good Friday Agreement

prerequisites: Sustained political will in both capitals
Zero-tolerance for cross-border terrorism (Pakistan action on militant groups)
India's acceptance of third-party mediation on Kashmir
14_imaginary_peace_scenario.inspirational_quote: If Europe can unify after World Wars, India and Pakistan can reconcile after Partition. Peace is not just the absence of war, but the presence of prosperity for 1.6 billion people yearning for it. - Adapted from regional peace advocates

interventions.title: Afghanistan War (2001-2021)
interventions.description: US invasion of Afghanistan post-9/11 forced Pakistan to become frontline state against Taliban (despite Pakistan's covert support). India supported Northern Alliance. US aid to Pakistan: $33 billion (2001-2020). Created India-Pakistan proxy war in Afghanistan.
interventions.source: US Special Inspector General for Afghanistan Reconstruction (SIGAR), Congressional Research Service

interventions.title: F-16 Sales to Pakistan
interventions.description: US supplied Pakistan with F-16 fighter jets despite India's objections. Latest: 8 F-16s approved in 2016 ($699M). Shifted balance of air power in South Asia.
interventions.source: US Defense Security Cooperation Agency, Stockholm International Peace Research Institute

actual_outcomes: Pakistan became nuclear power with US tacit approval (1998)
India-Pakistan wars averted by US mediation (1999 Kargil, 2001-02 standoff)
Afghanistan remains unstable; Taliban rule since 2021
alternative_scenario_without_us: Scenario A: No Afghan War → No Taliban rise → Pakistan not destabilized by militancy
Scenario B: India-Pakistan left to resolve Kashmir bilaterally → Potential SCO mediation
Scenario C: No US arms to Pakistan → India's conventional military superiority leads to earlier resolution
Quantified: US aid ($33B to Pakistan) could have funded 5,000 km of cross-border rail if spent on peace

regional_autonomy.sco_mediation_potential: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (Russia-China led) could mediate Kashmir if US withdrew; more acceptable to Pakistan than Western mediation
regional_autonomy.saarc_revival: Without US influence, SAARC could function as EU-like framework with joint Kashmir administration
